Development:
Genetic Model Systems and the Characterization of Genes that Determine Body Plan
C. elegans develops in a specific lineage pathway

Important Concepts Regarding Drosophila Developmental Genetics
Drosophila Develops in a sequential, predictable pattern with embryonic, larval, pupal and adult stages.
Many mutants have been isolated that identify the proteins and their functions that are responsible for specific transitions.
These genes are conserved, and play a major role in human development

Drosophila Development
Not strictly Cell-lineage Development
Similar to vertebrates
